MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle McGill for Lois Farrell. Richard Canatella for Richard Farrell and Donald Farrell (Petitioners).

The Court stated that the Order After Hearing signed by Commissioner Kaplan on May 26, 2009 ordered Donald Farrell to provide and file a complete accounting in the form required by the probate code by June 12, 2009. Said accounting is to be filed by August 13, 2010. Status for filing of Donald Farrell's accounting was continued to August 18, 2010 at 10:30 a.m, in Dept. 204. Mr. Canatella stated that he did not have the opportunity to read Ms. McGill's objection, argued that they are entitled to an evidentiary hearing and presented oral argument to support his position. Ms. McGill argued that the Court cannot hear the petition for reformation as it is barred by collateral estoppel, that the petition raises the same issue with a different theory and that the petition is asking the Court to reconsider a matter that has already been decided. The Court stated that it was inclined to entertain Mr. Canatella's response to Ms. McGill's objection which is due by August 13, 2010. The Petition to Reform the Trust and the Petition for Order Directing Payment of Funds in Blocked Account were also continued to August 18, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Off Calendar Apr-29-2010 Continued To Jun-02-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle McGill and Edward Otonello for and with Lois Farrell. Richard Canatella for Donald Farrell and Richard Farrell (Petitioners/Respondents).
Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the tentative ruling on the 170.6 challenge to Commissioner Sue Kaplan, timeliness, lack of notice of Ms. McGill's intent to argue, the opportunity for Mr. Canatella to respond to Ms. McGill's memo and this Court having authority to rule on the Motion for Reconsideration. The Court stated that this Court needed to hear the Motion for Reconsideration because it follows on from the April 27, 2009 ruling on the first cause of action on the underlying petition which was to grant the petition. With regard to all of the other motions, the Court adopted the tentative as follows: Grant the CCP 170.6 challenge. All other motions/pending matters are to be re-scheduled before another Judicial Officer. The Court denied the Motion for Reconsideration stating that it is mooted by what the Appellate and Supreme Court has decided. Mr. Canatella stipulated to the Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings going off calendar. All of the remaining matters were continued to June 2, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard by Judge Wiss. The remaining matters to be heard are 1) The petition filed on June 19, 2008 with regard to the removal of the Trustee and the accounting. 2) The Motion for Sanctions filed on August 7, 2009 by Ms. McGill. 3) The Petition for Order to Set Aside Non-Final Order filed on January 4, 2010 by Mr. Canatella. 4) The Motion for Sanctions filed on January 13, 2010 by Mr. Canatella 5) The Petition for Order Directing Payment of Funds in the Blocked Account to Lois Farrell filed on March 8, 2010 by Ms. McGill. 6) The Motion to Dismiss/Demurrer filed on March 16, 2010 by Mr. Canatella. Ms. McGill will prepare an order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Off Calendar Jun-29-2009 Continued To Nov-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for Donald Farrell (Trustee). Michelle McGill for Lois Farrell (Petitioner).

The Parties presented oral argument to support their postions with regard to the stay issue, Lois Farrell having standing, the removal of Trustee Donald Farrell, the fiduciary's responsibilities, the accounting, the appeal and the identification of the trust assets. Upon conclusion of argument, Mr. Canatella stipulated to depositing the amount of $181,756.69 into a blocked account for the benefit of Lois Farrell. In addition, Mr. Canatella stipulated that no other distributions are to be made of any kind to any person. Ms. McGill will prepare the order and present it to Mr. Canatella. Status of filing of proof of the blocked account was set for July 13, 2009. The Petition To Remove Trustee was continued to August 3, 2009. The Motion For Reconsideration and the Accounting are stayed pending the appeal and continued to November 23, 2009. The Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings on calendar for July 9, 2009 was also continued to November 23, 2009 and Ms. McGill's Motion for Sanctions will also be heard on November 23, 2009.

The Court confirmed the results of the appeal and took the status on the appeal off calendar. Ms. McGill presented oral argument to support her position. The Court found that Manuel Farrel is a beneficiary of the trust and that the surviving spouse Lois has standing as a successor in interest. Mr. Canatella was directed to file the accounting by June 12, 2009. Objections to the accounting are due by June 15, 2009. A status hearing on the accounting was set for June 29, 2009. The issue regarding the removal of Donald Farrell as Trustee was continued to June 29, 2009.

Notice Of Hearing Demurrer And Motion To Strike; Verified Demurrer Of Donald Farrell To Verified Petition Of Lois Farrell;
Off Calendar Aug-07-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Successor Trustee Donald Farrell; Michele McGill and Ed Ottonello for Petitioner Lois Farrell.

Mr. Canatella made argument with relevant facts to the Demurrer and Motion to Strike of trustee Donald Farrell, with opposition made by Mr. Ottonello.
After hearing the arguments and considering the points and authorities submitted in support of and in opposition to the demurrer and motion to strike, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The Demurrer is overruled. Trustee Donald Farrell has ten days from the date of service of the Order to answer the Petition. The Motion to Stricken is denied. The Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Order singed in open court.
